description: Pilot on the hard puzzle: puzzle_002 (7x7 hard, Puzzle-loop #25425, unique solution verified) across 4 toolsets, meta-off, n=1, max_turns=200.
motivation: Check whether the easy-puzzle cost ordering holds on a genuinely hard instance and whether ratios are stable with difficulty.
formal_results: All solved. none $5.28/2 calls/1776s; fine $4.99/124; medium $3.61/51; coarse $2.32/51/613s. coarse/none = 0.44x. none flips from cheapest (easy) to most expensive (hard); fine call-count scales with edges (65 -> 124).
results_description: Pure reasoning (none) becomes the costliest toolset on hard puzzles while coarse stays cheapest; the cost gap widens vs the easy puzzle.
conclusions: Supports a difficulty-dependent granularity benefit and the none cheap->expensive flip - but n=1, so pilot-quality only.
